summary:
Benralizumab is not effective for chronic spontaneous urticaria due to a fundamental mechanistic mismatch; it targets IL-5-mediated eosinophilia, while CSU is driven by mast cell activation and histamine release. No clinical evidence supports its use in CSU, making it inappropriate as a treatment compared to established therapies like omalizumab.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to two sentences and focuses only on clinically relevant facts.)
